description: Construct a sampling distribution by repeatedly drawing with-replacement resamples from the observed data — a nonparametric approach to inference that requires no distributional assumption

fields.name: observed_sample
fields.label: Array of observed data points — treated as the empirical distribution

fields.name: statistic
fields.label: mean | median | variance | std_dev | quantile | correlation | custom

fields.name: confidence_level
fields.label: Confidence level for percentile / BCa interval (e.g., 0.95)

core_principle.treat_sample_as_population: The observed sample is treated as if it were the population; resamples mimic repeated draws from that population
core_principle.with_replacement: Each bootstrap resample is drawn WITH replacement, same size n as original
core_principle.items_may_repeat: An observation can appear multiple times in a resample; others may not appear at all

process.step_1: Treat observed sample as the empirical distribution F_hat
process.step_2: Draw a bootstrap resample of size n WITH replacement from F_hat
process.step_3: Compute the statistic on the resample
process.step_5: Use the B replicate statistics as an approximation to the true sampling distribution

inference_outputs.standard_error: Bootstrap SE = sample std dev of the B replicate statistics
inference_outputs.bca_ci: Bias-corrected and accelerated CI improves coverage for skewed statistics

source_of_randomness.monte_carlo: Random draws from a SPECIFIED parametric distribution
source_of_randomness.bootstrap: Random draws from the OBSERVED empirical distribution (no parametric assumption)

when_to_use_each.use_bootstrap: Historical sample is rich and representative; no good parametric model
when_to_use_each.use_monte_carlo: Parametric model is theoretically justified or empirical data is sparse

strengths.distribution_free: No normality or other parametric assumption required
strengths.handles_arbitrary_statistics: Works for statistics (median, quantiles, ratios) where analytic SEs are difficult
strengths.simple_to_implement: Requires only resampling and evaluating the statistic

limitations.sample_dependent: Resamples inherit any bias or incompleteness of the original sample
limitations.poor_tail_estimation: Cannot extrapolate beyond observed range — tail events absent from the sample stay absent
limitations.iid_assumption: Standard bootstrap assumes i.i.d. observations; time series need block bootstrap

applications.standard_errors: SE of median, Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and other complex statistics
applications.hypothesis_tests: Permutation tests via bootstrap under the null
applications.confidence_intervals: Percentile / BCa intervals for performance metrics
applications.portfolio_backtesting: Bootstrap historical returns to assess strategy robustness

run_bootstrap.description: Execute B resamples and return the bootstrap sampling distribution

compute_confidence_interval.description: Derive percentile or BCa CI from the bootstrap distribution

empty_sample.description: Observed sample missing or too small

errors.message: Number of resamples must be at least 100 (recommended >= 1000)
Observed sample must contain at least 2 observations

anti_patterns.rule: Do not bootstrap i.i.d. samples from a time series with autocorrelation
anti_patterns.why: Standard bootstrap destroys temporal dependence; resampled series have spurious i.i.d. properties. Use block bootstrap or stationary bootstrap for dependent data

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on the bootstrap for tail-risk estimation
anti_patterns.why: The bootstrap cannot generate events beyond the observed range; using it for 99.9% VaR systematically understates tail loss if the sample lacks crisis observations

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore the finite-sample bias of the statistic being bootstrapped
anti_patterns.why: Bootstrap estimates the sampling distribution around the sample value, not the population value; bias correction (BCa, jackknife-after-bootstrap) is essential for skewed statistics

anti_patterns.rule: Do not conflate the bootstrap with Monte Carlo simulation
anti_patterns.why: Bootstrap draws from empirical data; Monte Carlo draws from an assumed parametric distribution. Confusing them leads to incorrect model selection and false confidence in results

goals.description: Construct a sampling distribution by repeatedly drawing with-replacement resamples from the observed data — a nonparametric approach to inference that requires no distributional assumption

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

worked_example.scenario: Bootstrap the sample mean of 30 returns
steps: Draw 30 returns with replacement from the original sample
Compute the mean of this resample
Sample SD of the 1,000 means = bootstrap SE of the mean
worked_example.comparison_to_clt: Bootstrap SE approximates analytic SE = s / sqrt(n); advantage is it also works for median, Sharpe, etc.

time_series_variant.solution: Block bootstrap: resample contiguous blocks of length b (b ~ n^(1/3))
time_series_variant.preserves: Short-range dependence structure of the series
